depends_on: RESOLUTION_WATCHER_INTEGRATE_001 must be APPROVED — both touch bot_core.py
purpose: Harden the runtime execution-side risk monitoring loop. The exit loop must reliably close positions when TP/SL/gap conditions fire, surface the correct held-side price on every iteration, handle stale/None OB gracefully without silently blocking exit, and log every close event with full context. This phase does NOT redesign thresholds — it ensures the monitoring loop is airtight.

in_scope: bot_core.py — exit loop (lines ~540-567 at time of audit), _load_resolved_markets(), _dummy_market(), _market_by_id()
core/risk.py — check_exit() robustness, _held_bid() None handling, SL cluster detection
out_of_scope: core/paper_exec.py — do not touch in this phase
Do not change TP/SL threshold values
Do not redesign the exit reason taxonomy (near_resolution, stop_loss, gap_stop, etc.)
Do not touch mlb_model/ or recommendation_api.py

monitoring_invariants_to_enforce.description: The following behaviors must hold after this phase.

rules.description: If check_exit() returns (False, '') because current_held_price is None (empty OB), the exit loop must log a WARNING — not silently skip. The operator must be able to see that a position is stuck due to missing OB data.
rules.fix: After check_exit returns (False, ''), check if ob.micro_reason == 'empty_book' or _held_bid() would return None — if so, emit logger.warning('Exit check skipped trade=%d reason=empty_ob', trade.id)

rules.description: Every CLOSE event must log: trade.id, market_slug, reason, exit_px, pnl_usd. No silent closes.
rules.verify: Confirm the logger.info('CLOSE trade=...') line runs for every exit path including market_resolved

rules.description: If the orderbook is stale/empty AND the last known price was below the SL threshold, the bot must not indefinitely defer the exit. Define a max stale duration after which a time-based SL forces the close.
rules.fix: If _held_bid() is None and time since trade open > STALE_OB_MAX_SECONDS (configurable, default 300), emit a warning. Implementation of forced exit is optional in this phase — warning emission is mandatory.

rules.description: _dummy_market() creates a market object with empty token IDs. Any trade using a dummy market in the exit loop must be logged at WARNING level so the operator knows market discovery failed for this position.
rules.fix: Add logger.warning('Exit using dummy market for trade=%d market_id=%s — no live discovery match', trade.id, trade.market_id) when _dummy_market() is used

rules.description: All cooldown assignments (_market_cooldown[market_id] = ...) must use identical log format. Current format confirmed for near_resolution, stop_loss, gap_stop — verify trailing_stop and market_resolved do not set cooldowns (they should not).
rules.verify: Confirm no cooldown is set for take_profit, trailing_stop, market_resolved, time_exit

rules.description: The except block at the exit loop must log the full exception with trade.id and exc_info=True, not just a warning. Operator must be able to diagnose exit loop failures from logs alone.
rules.fix: Change logger.warning('Exit check error trade=%s: %s') to logger.error('Exit check error trade=%s: %s', trade.id, e, exc_info=True)

result_must_include: rules_enforced: list of rule IDs confirmed or fixed
rules_partial: list of rule IDs only partially addressed with explanation
verification: py_compile passes, git diff --name-only matches allowed_files

preflight_questions: Is RESOLUTION_WATCHER_INTEGRATE_001 APPROVED? (bot_core.py must not have two concurrent writers)
Read the phase 0 audit report's 'immediate_risks' section before writing any code
What does the current exit loop exception handler look like? (Confirm it is logger.warning before changing to logger.error)
Does _held_bid() currently return None on empty OB or raise? (Check core/risk.py)
acceptance_criteria: All 6 monitoring invariants above are enforced or documented as PARTIAL with justification
Empty OB / None held_price logs a WARNING and does not silently skip
Exit loop exception is logged at ERROR with exc_info=True
Only files in allowed_files were changed
No TP/SL threshold values were changed
proof_of_work: Show git diff for each changed file
For each invariant rule, show: (a) the old code, (b) the new code, (c) the log line it produces
Confirm via grep that logger.warning('Exit check error') is replaced by logger.error
guardrails: Do not redesign strategy or model logic
Do not change TP/SL threshold values
Do not reintroduce ambiguous YES/NO economics
Held-side contract truth must remain the basis for pricing and exit logic
Do not touch dashboard_server.py or core/paper_exec.py in this phase
Use localhost:8900 as dashboard target if any manual smoke verification is needed
